mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy. This strength translates into higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns as investors price in continued economic health.
evidence_refs: fact:The post-2009 economic recovery featured steady PAYEMS growth alongside a prolonged equity bull market.
failure_condition: A rolling 36-month analysis shows a statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) or negative correlation between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a short period is a strong real-time indicator of a recessionary shock. This signals a sharp deterioration in the economic outlook, causing a flight to safety and negative returns for equities.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month low.
evidence_refs: fact:Sharp increases in UNRATE preceded major market downturns in 2001 and 2008.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts positive total returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) remains static, the central bank is perceived as being 'behind the curve'. This creates uncertainty about future, more aggressive tightening and erodes real returns, leading to negative equity performance.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change > 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change is between -0.10pp and +0.10pp.
evidence_refs: fact:Periods in the 1970s demonstrated that equities perform poorly when inflation accelerates and monetary policy fails to respond promptly.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of this combined signal for 60-day forward returns is not statistically different from zero over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) serves as a leading indicator of a broader slowdown in consumer and business investment. This signals weakening aggregate demand, negatively impacting future corporate earnings and equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:A collapse in housing starts preceded the 2007-2008 financial crisis and equity market crash.
failure_condition: The signal has a hit rate below 50% for predicting negative 60-day returns during periods identified as late-cycle (e.g., UNRATE < 4.5% and expansion > 5 years).

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a healthy and growing supply side of the economy. This is directly linked to the revenue and profit growth of manufacturing and industrial companies, supporting positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production growth was a key feature of the economic expansion phases in the 1990s and 2010s.
failure_condition: The median 20-day forward S&P 500 return following months with a top-quintile INDPRO change is not statistically higher than the overall median return.

mechanism_narrative: An upside surprise in monthly retail sales (RSAFS) indicates stronger-than-expected consumer spending, which constitutes the largest part of the US economy. This leads to immediate upward revisions of revenue forecasts and positive short-term equity market reactions.
evidence_refs: fact:Market commentary frequently attributes short-term rallies to strong retail sales data releases.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return on days when the signal is triggered is not positive and statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05) over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of declining industrial production (INDPRO) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a stagflationary environment. This is exceptionally damaging for equities, as it compresses corporate profit margins and reduces real economic growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year change < 0% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 3%
evidence_refs: doc:NBER working papers on the 1970s stagflation
failure_condition: A strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when this signal is active fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over any 10-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A 'precautionary' cut in the FEDFUNDS rate when the labor market is already at or near full employment (low UNRATE) reveals the Federal Reserve's concern about a significant, non-obvious downside risk to the economy, leading to negative forward returns as the market prices in this new risk.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve cut rates three times in 2019 while unemployment was near 50-year lows, citing global risks and muted inflation.
failure_condition: Instances of Fed rate cuts below the 4.0% unemployment threshold are followed by positive median 20-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of cases.
